items.title: Warren Buffett Successor Greg Abel Made 3 Big Purchases Last Quarter, and They're All Exceptional Performers
items.summary: Greg Abel, the new CEO of Berkshire Hathaway, made three major investments in Q1 2026 totaling billions in capital. He acquired OxyChem from Occidental Petroleum for $9.7 billion, invested $1.8 billion in Japanese insurer Tokio Marine, and tripled Berkshire's position in Alphabet with an $11 billion investment. All three investments have performed exceptionally well so far.

items.title: Does Warren Buffett's Successor, Greg Abel, Know Something That Wall Street Doesn't? He's Piling Into a "Magnificent Seven" Stock at Close to 30x Earnings That Other Billionaire Hedge Fund Managers Are Dumping.
items.summary: Greg Abel, the new CEO of Berkshire Hathaway, has more than tripled the company's position in Alphabet in Q1 2026, making it the fifth-largest holding at 6.7% of the portfolio. This move contrasts with other prominent billionaire investors like Bill Ackman and Stanley Druckenmiller who are reducing their Alphabet stakes. Abel's aggressive bet on Alphabet appears to be a strategic play on the company's AI capabilities and dominant market position, despite concerns about high capital expenditures impacting free cash flow.

items.title: SpaceX’s Next Mission Leads to Wall Street
items.summary: SpaceX is preparing for a historic IPO on Nasdaq in June 2026 at a $1.75 trillion valuation, the largest IPO ever. The company dominates global launch markets with 80% market share and operates Starlink with 9 million subscribers generating $11.4 billion in revenue. However, the merger with xAI creates financial pressure with significant AI infrastructure losses, and the company faces governance concerns with Musk controlling 93.6% of voting power. Success depends on achieving profitability in Starlink and orbital data center ventures.

items.title: Stock Market Today, May 29: Microsoft Rises as $37 Billion AI Run Rate Highlights Cloud Growth
items.summary: Microsoft stock surged 5.45% to $450.24 after announcing its AI business reached a $37 billion annual revenue run rate, demonstrating strong cloud and software monetization. The company is also developing in-house AI models to control costs while maintaining its partnership with OpenAI. Meanwhile, peers Apple and Alphabet underperformed, with the broader S&P 500 and Nasdaq gaining modestly.

items.title: Google (GOOGL) Engineer Faces Federal Charges Over Alleged $1.2 Million Polymarket Insider Trading Scheme - foreignpolicyjournal.com
items.summary: Google (GOOGL) Engineer Faces Federal Charges Over Alleged $1.2 Million Polymarket Insider Trading Scheme    foreignpolicyjournal.com

items.title: 5 Warren Buffett Stocks to Hold Forever
items.summary: The article highlights five Berkshire Hathaway holdings recommended as long-term investments: American Express, Alphabet, Apple, Coca-Cola, and Moody's. These stocks are praised for their consistent performance, strong business models, and market dominance in their respective industries. New CEO Greg Abel continues to maintain these positions, signaling confidence in their long-term value.

items.title: The SpaceX IPO Is Coming Next Month: Here's What You Need to Know
items.summary: SpaceX filed its IPO prospectus, revealing steady 33% revenue growth in 2025 driven by Starlink's $11.4B revenue and 10.3M subscribers. However, the company reported a $4.9B loss in 2025, primarily due to massive spending by its xAI division on GPU infrastructure and data centers. While Starlink is profitable with $1.2B operating income, the rocket and AI businesses operate at losses. Elon Musk will retain 85% voting control through a dual-class share structure.

items.title: 3 AI Stocks Worth Holding Until You Retire
items.summary: The article recommends three AI-focused stocks as long-term retirement holdings: Palantir Technologies for its advanced AI platform solving complex problems with accelerating growth; CrowdStrike for leading the cybersecurity market with strong recurring revenue and AI-driven demand; and Alphabet for its competitive moat in AI infrastructure and ability to monetize AI across consumer and enterprise services.

items.title: CHAT vs. XLK: CHAT Outperforms XLK on Returns, But XLK Offers on Fees
items.summary: Roundhill Investments' Generative AI & Technology ETF (CHAT) has delivered superior returns of 112.7% over one year with a 54.7% CAGR since inception, significantly outpacing State Street's Technology Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLK) at 52.5% annual returns. However, CHAT charges a 0.75% expense ratio compared to XLK's 0.08%, making XLK more cost-efficient. CHAT suits aggressive AI-focused investors willing to accept higher volatility, while XLK appeals to those seeking broad tech exposure with lower fees and established stability.

items.title: Assessing Alphabet (GOOGL) Valuation As EU Penalty Threat And AI Infrastructure Spend Draw Focus - simplywall.st
items.summary: Assessing Alphabet (GOOGL) Valuation As EU Penalty Threat And AI Infrastructure Spend Draw Focus    simplywall.st

items.summary: EU Reportedly Plans to Fine Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL.US) Hundreds of Millions of EUR Over Search Engine Self-Preferencing    AASTOCKS.com

items.title: Alphabet (GOOGL) Slips Near $383 as AI Capex, Cloud Growth, and Search Reinvention Divide Wall Street - FXLeaders
items.summary: Alphabet (GOOGL) Slips Near $383 as AI Capex, Cloud Growth, and Search Reinvention Divide Wall Street    FXLeaders

items.title: Warren Buffett's Favorite Holdings: 3 Stocks Worth Owning for a Lifetime
items.summary: Warren Buffett's three favorite stocks—Apple, Coca-Cola, and American Express—are highlighted as lifetime holdings worth owning. Apple benefits from its ecosystem and strong iPhone sales, Coca-Cola demonstrates resilience with a 64-year dividend streak, and American Express differentiates itself through its closed-loop model and membership fees. New CEO Greg Abel appears committed to maintaining these positions.

items.title: 3 Ridiculously Cheap AI Stocks That Could Turn $5,000 Into at Least $10,000 by 2028
items.summary: The article highlights three AI stocks with growth potential: Alphabet leverages AI across its business with strong Google Cloud growth; Silicon Motion Technology produces NAND flash controllers with impressive sequential revenue growth; and Marvell Technology provides critical data center solutions for AI infrastructure. All three companies show strong financial metrics and positive guidance.

items.title: Consumer Tech News (May 18-22): Meta Cuts 8,000 Jobs, Nvidia Posts Strong Earnings & More
items.summary: Major tech companies reported mixed results this week. Nvidia posted strong Q1 earnings with $81.6B revenue, beating expectations. Meta announced layoffs of 8,000 employees (10% of workforce) and canceled 6,000 open positions. Other highlights include Anthropic's path to profitability, Alphabet and Blackstone forming an AI data center venture, and various AI infrastructure developments across the industry.

items.title: Weekend Round-Up: Nvidia's Q1 Triumph, SpaceX's IPO Filing, Musk's OpenAI Controversy, Google's AI Leap And More
items.summary: Tech sector dominated headlines this week with Nvidia reporting stellar Q1 earnings of $81.6B (85% YoY growth), SpaceX filing for IPO with potential $1.75T valuation, Google appealing antitrust ruling while unveiling new AI products at I/O 2026, and the Trump administration awarding $2B to quantum computing companies. ARM Holdings surged 170% in 2026 following Nvidia's strong results.

items.title: 37.4% of Berkshire Hathaway's $330 Billion Portfolio Is Parked in 3 Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks
items.summary: Despite not chasing trends, Berkshire Hathaway has over one-third of its $330 billion portfolio invested in three AI-focused stocks: Apple (20.7% weighting), Alphabet (6.8% weighting), and Coca-Cola. Under new CEO Greg Abel, Berkshire nearly tripled its Alphabet position in Q1 2026. All three companies are leveraging AI to enhance their core businesses—Apple through Apple Intelligence features, Alphabet through AI-enhanced Google Search generating record revenue, and Coca-Cola through AI-driven product development and cloud partnerships.

items.title: This "Magnificent Seven" Stock Is the Worst Performer of 2026. Is It Finally a Buy?
items.summary: Microsoft is the worst-performing Magnificent Seven stock in 2026, down 13% despite accelerating business fundamentals. The company's fiscal Q3 revenue grew 18% with AI products generating a $37 billion annual run rate, but massive capital expenditures ($190 billion expected in 2026) are pressuring margins. Trading at a relatively low forward P/E of 22, Microsoft may offer value for long-term investors willing to tolerate near-term risks from its costly AI infrastructure build-out.

items.title: Berkshire Hathaway Energy Could Be a Quiet Star of the Greg Abel Era
items.summary: Under new CEO Greg Abel, Berkshire Hathaway Energy remains a reliable cash cow for the conglomerate, generating $1.1 billion in Q1 2026 operating earnings. The utility business provides stable, consistent profits regardless of economic conditions and is positioned to benefit from growing AI data center power demands. With $400 billion in idle cash, Berkshire may increasingly invest in expanding this proven business.

items.title: Sun Financial Inc Acquires New Shares in Alphabet Inc. $GOOGL - MarketBeat
items.summary: Sun Financial Inc Acquires New Shares in Alphabet Inc. $GOOGL    MarketBeat

items.title: The AI Stock Built for Investors Who Don't Want to Trade -- Just Compound
items.summary: Alphabet is positioned as a strong AI investment for long-term compounding, with Q1 operating income jumping 30% and Search revenue growing at its fastest rate in four years. The company has reduced AI response costs by 30% through hardware and engineering improvements, while Google Cloud operating profit surged 203% year-over-year. However, the company's heavy reliance on advertising revenue (70%) presents vulnerability during economic downturns.

items.title: Alphabet Inc. $GOOGL Shares Acquired by Reynders McVeigh Capital Management LLC - MarketBeat

items.title: SpaceX Scarcity Trade Risks Turning a Great Company Into a Bad Entry
items.summary: SpaceX's anticipated Nasdaq IPO at a $1.75 trillion valuation presents both significant opportunity and hype-driven risks. While the company has strong fundamentals in Starlink (61% of 2025 revenue), reusable launch systems, and government contracts, investors must contend with heavy AI losses ($4.9B loss on $18.7B revenue in 2025), governance risks from Elon Musk's control, and a valuation that assumes successful execution across multiple difficult fronts including Starship development and AI infrastructure profitability.

items.title: NVIDIA (NVDA) and Alphabet (GOOGL) Develop Cost-Effective AI Sol - GuruFocus
items.summary: NVIDIA (NVDA) and Alphabet (GOOGL) Develop Cost-Effective AI Sol    GuruFocus

items.title: The AI Token Pricing Crisis Behind OpenAI and Anthropic’s Revenue Race
items.summary: OpenAI and Anthropic are experiencing rapid revenue growth, but enterprise customers are hitting budget ceilings due to soaring AI token costs. While OpenAI generated $5.7B in Q1 2026 revenue, Anthropic's annualized growth trajectory is faster. However, major customers like Uber burned through their entire 2026 AI budget in four months, forcing companies to consolidate vendors and reconsider AI spending. The crisis stems from expensive GPU infrastructure costs, though long-term hardware efficiency improvements and Google's cheaper Gemini models may eventually ease the pressure.

items.title: Audiencerate: Riccardo Fabbri wird Chief Technology Officer. Die KI-gesteuerte Phase der Plattformen für KMU und Medienagenturen beginnt
items.summary: Audiencerate appoints Riccardo Fabbri, former co-founder and managing partner of Nohup (acquired by Havas in 2021), as Chief Technology Officer. Fabbri will lead AI infrastructure development integrating first-party and third-party data to support platforms for Italian SMEs (via Postel and Microsoft partnership) and media agencies (with Google DV360 integration).

items.title: Audiencerate : Riccardo Fabbri rejoint l’équipe en tant que directeur technique. Début de la phase pilotée par l’IA des plateformes pour PME et agences médias
items.summary: Audiencerate Ltd announced the appointment of Riccardo Fabbri as Chief Technology Officer to lead the development of AI-powered infrastructure. Fabbri, former co-founder of Nohup (acquired by Havas in 2021), will oversee the expansion of two platforms: one for Italian SMEs in partnership with Postel and Microsoft, and another integrated with Google DV360 for media agencies worldwide, both leveraging AI and machine learning for data activation and audience modeling.

items.title: Audiencerate: Riccardo Fabbri Joins as Chief Technology Officer. The AI-Driven Phase of the Platforms for SMEs and Media Agencies Begins
items.summary: Audiencerate Ltd announced the appointment of Riccardo Fabbri, co-founder of Nohup (acquired by Havas Group in 2021), as Chief Technology Officer. Fabbri will lead the development of AI and machine learning infrastructure for two platforms: one for Italian SMEs in partnership with Postel and Microsoft, and another integrated with Google DV360 for media agencies. The appointment marks the beginning of the company's AI-driven expansion phase.

items.title: Audiencerate: Riccardo Fabbri Chief Technology Officer. Al via la fase AI-driven delle piattaforme per PMI e agenzie media
items.summary: Audiencerate Ltd announces Riccardo Fabbri, co-founder of Nohup (acquired by Havas in 2021), as Chief Technology Officer. Fabbri will lead the development of AI-powered infrastructure integrating first and third-party data across two platforms: one for Italian SMEs via Postel and Microsoft partnership, and another for media agencies integrated with Google DV360. The appointment marks the beginning of an AI-driven expansion phase leveraging machine learning for predictive modeling and audience intelligence.

items.title: Blackstone and Google Forge an AI Empire as AI Consolidation Begins
items.summary: Blackstone and Google announced a $5 billion joint venture to create an AI cloud platform with 500 megawatts of capacity by 2027, using equity financing and Google's proprietary TPUs. This move signals a consolidation phase in AI infrastructure, favoring mega-cap players with strong balance sheets over debt-heavy mid-tier competitors. The partnership poses structural challenges to pure-play AI infrastructure operators like CoreWeave and Nebius Group, which face margin pressures and valuation risks respectively.

items.title: TD Cowen Highlights Waymo Growth at Alphabet (GOOGL) - Insider Monkey
items.summary: TD Cowen Highlights Waymo Growth at Alphabet (GOOGL)    Insider Monkey

items.title: 1 Unstoppable Stock to Buy Before It Joins Nvidia, Alphabet, Apple, and Microsoft in the $3 Trillion Club
items.summary: Micron Technology is positioned to join the $3 trillion market cap club as AI adoption drives surging demand for memory and storage chips. The company reported record revenue of $23.9 billion in Q2 FY2026 with 196% year-over-year growth and projects Q3 revenue of $33.5 billion. With a current market cap of $784 billion and trading at an attractive 12x forward earnings, Micron could potentially reach $3 trillion by 2030 if it achieves Wall Street's projected 41% annual growth.

items.title: What's Going On With Blackstone Stock Tuesday?
items.summary: Blackstone announced a joint venture with Google to build AI infrastructure and cloud computing services powered by Google Cloud's TPUs. Blackstone will commit $5 billion in initial equity, with plans to bring 500 megawatts of capacity online in 2027. The partnership aims to meet growing demand for accelerated computing in AI development. BX stock traded up 0.82% in premarket trading, though technical analysis shows bearish signals with the stock trading below key moving averages.

items.title: Anthropic Buys Stainless, Acquiring Startup Behind OpenAI's Developer Libraries
items.summary: Anthropic has acquired Stainless, a developer tools startup founded in 2022, for approximately $300 million to improve developer experience and enhance connections between AI agents and external systems. Stainless will wind down its products including its SDK generator. The acquisition comes as Anthropic reportedly seeks new funding at an $800 billion valuation and has seen its annualized revenue reach $30 billion in April, surpassing OpenAI's $24 billion run rate.

items.title: AI Boom Resembles the Internet Rally as Markets Party Like 1999
items.summary: The article compares the current AI boom to the 1999 internet rally, highlighting strong growth prospects for AI and data center stocks. Nvidia is expected to deliver impressive quarterly results with 79.7% sales growth and 119.8% earnings growth. The author recommends staying fully invested to capitalize on this once-in-a-lifetime opportunity, with data center order backlogs expected to sustain momentum through 2027. A potential Samsung labor strike could worsen memory chip shortages.

items.title: Prediction: This Beaten-Down Tech Stock Will Outperform the S&P 500 by 2027
items.summary: Reddit stock has declined over 30% year-to-date despite strong fundamentals including 17% year-over-year growth in daily active users (126.8M), 69% revenue growth, and a 30.8% net profit margin in Q1 2026. The article argues concerns about AI search engines cannibalizing Reddit traffic are overblown, as the platform's unique user-generated content and AI licensing deals position it well for future growth.

items.title: Alphabet's New Growth Engine Is Something Investors Can't Miss
items.summary: Alphabet has begun directly selling its Tensor Processing Units (TPUs) to select clients, significantly boosting Google Cloud's growth. In Q1, Google Cloud revenue surged 63% year-over-year with operating margins improving from 19% to 33%. This positions Alphabet as a major player in AI hardware and cloud computing, with the company's overall Q1 revenue and operating income growing 22% and 30% respectively.

items.title: Kettering University Names Alumnus Qasar Younis, Co-founder and CEO of Applied Intuition, as 2026 Commencement Speaker
items.summary: Kettering University has selected Qasar Younis, co-founder and CEO of Applied Intuition, as the 2026 Commencement speaker and will award him an honorary Doctor of Engineering degree. Younis, a 2004 alumnus, leads a prominent AI company focused on physical AI for autonomous systems across automotive, defense, and industrial sectors. The university highlighted how Younis's career exemplifies Kettering's engineering education model combined with professional industry experience.

items.title: This AI Stock Has Every Quality Warren Buffett Looks for in a Long-Term Hold
items.summary: Microsoft is highlighted as an AI stock that embodies Warren Buffett's investment philosophy, featuring a strong competitive moat through its enterprise software dominance, reliable cash flow generation, and a 21-year track record of dividend increases. Despite a rough start to 2026, the company remains an attractive long-term investment for those following Buffett's principles.

items.title: Veterans For American Update: CLJA Veterans Pool Issues (Mo’ Money More Problems)
items.summary: A Colorado bankruptcy judge foreclosed what may be the largest Chapter 11 case in state history involving 440,040 veteran members of the Camp Lejeune Justice Act Settlement after a critical court filing missed the May 1 deadline. The case is being reopened following recovery of the misdirected package. VetWork leadership is pursuing statutory rights to reopen the case and plans a major CLJA veterans awareness conference in Denver this summer.

items.title: Great News: This Magnificent 7 Stock May Still Have Lots of Room to Run
items.summary: Alphabet is capitalizing on AI demand across Google Cloud, Gemini, Search, and enterprise tools, with strong quarterly momentum. However, the company's massive capital expenditure plans raise concerns about whether it can sustain earnings growth without compromising free cash flow.

items.title: 2 AI Stocks to Avoid (Including BigBear.ai) and 1 to Buy Now
items.summary: BigBear.ai and C3.ai are losing relevance in the crowded AI software market due to declining revenues, widening losses, and intense competition. BigBear.ai's revenue declined from $146M to $128M (2021-2025) with net losses expanding to $294M, while C3.ai faces projected revenue decline from $389M to $251M (fiscal 2025-2028). In contrast, Broadcom is recommended as a superior AI play, with AI chip sales surging 65% to $20B in fiscal 2025 and projected to reach $60-90B by fiscal 2027, trading at a reasonable 18x next year's adjusted EBITDA.

items.title: 3 Monster Energy Stocks to Hold for the Next 10 Years
items.summary: The article recommends three energy stocks for long-term 10-year investment: Chevron, Williams Companies, and Brookfield Renewable. Chevron offers diversified upstream, midstream, and downstream operations with 39 years of consecutive dividend increases and expected 23% EPS CAGR through 2028. Williams Companies operates 33,000 miles of natural gas pipelines and benefits from AI data center demand growth with 11% EBITDA CAGR expected. Brookfield Renewable provides green energy solutions with 47 GW of operating capacity and 200 GW in pipeline, profiting from AI infrastructure and decarbonization trends.

items.title: OpenAI Merges ChatGPT And Codex, Taps Greg Brockman To Lead Product Strategy Ahead Of Potential IPO: Report
items.summary: OpenAI is consolidating its core products—ChatGPT, Codex AI coding agent, and developer API—under a unified product structure ahead of a potential IPO filing later this year. Greg Brockman has been given permanent responsibility for product strategy, while Thibault Sottiaux will lead the merged core product team. The restructuring follows the departure of several senior executives and positions OpenAI to compete more effectively against rivals Anthropic and Alphabet in AI markets.

items.title: Nvidia vs. Alphabet: Which Will Be the World's Biggest Company By the End of 2026?
items.summary: Nvidia currently holds the title of world's largest company with a $5.5 trillion market cap, but Alphabet is closing in at $4.8 trillion and has significantly outperformed Nvidia over the past year. Alphabet's cloud division is experiencing explosive growth with 63% revenue increase and 203% operating income growth, driven by AI demand. However, Nvidia benefits from this cloud expansion as it supplies the AI chips powering these services. Both companies are positioned as excellent long-term investments with strong competitive advantages in AI and cloud computing.

items.title: SpaceX IPO Prospectus Could Reportedly Drop Next Week—Here's A Peek Into What Investors May Be Buying At Astronomical Valuations
items.summary: SpaceX is preparing for a historic IPO targeting $70-75 billion in funding at a $1.75 trillion valuation. While Starlink's satellite internet business shows strong growth with 50% YoY revenue increase and 63% EBITDA margins, xAI's $9.5 billion cash burn in 2025 resulted in a $5 billion net loss. Governance concerns include Elon Musk's 79% voting control despite 42% equity stake. The prospectus is expected this week, with potential index inclusion driving significant passive buying pressure.

items.title: Meta's AI Chief Rejects 'Incorrect' Claims Of Mark Zuckerberg's Big-Money Talent Poaching: 'People Joined Because...'
items.summary: Meta's AI Chief Alexandr Wang dismissed claims that the company aggressively recruited top AI researchers with massive financial incentives, stating that researchers were attracted by access to high computing power and individualized recruiting processes rather than money. The article highlights Meta's intense competition for AI talent, including reports of CEO Mark Zuckerberg personally recruiting from OpenAI and Meta spending approximately $10 billion annually on AI talent.

items.title: Down 66%, Should You Buy the Dip in Rigetti Computing?
items.summary: Rigetti Computing stock has plummeted 66% from all-time highs after a speculative surge in 2025. Despite the pullback, the company's valuation remains unjustified with only $7.1 million in 2025 revenue against $216 million in net losses. With a P/S ratio of 607, structural operating losses of ~$100 million annually, and insider selling, the article warns investors that this falling stock may not represent a buying opportunity without significant commercial breakthroughs.

items.title: Berkshire's Cash Pile Might Go To Data Centers, But On Its Own Terms
items.summary: Berkshire Hathaway has accumulated a record $380.2 billion cash position while remaining a net seller of stocks for 14 consecutive quarters. Under new CEO Greg Abel, the company is positioning itself to capitalize on AI infrastructure buildout through data centers, but on strict terms requiring hyperscalers like Meta, Google, and Microsoft to bear full costs of new generation and transmission infrastructure rather than socializing expenses across ordinary utility customers.

items.title: 3 Stocks You'll Regret Not Buying Right Now
items.summary: The article recommends three stocks as buying opportunities despite recent market rallies: Nvidia, positioned to benefit from continued AI infrastructure investment with potential 50% upside; Micron Technology, thriving from memory chip shortages with explosive revenue growth; and Meta Platforms, trading cheaply at 19x forward earnings with strong ad business growth driven by AI improvements.

items.title: Meet the AI Stock Crushing Palantir, Nvidia, and Alphabet Right Now
items.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) has surged 115% year-to-date in 2026, significantly outperforming other AI stocks. The company reported strong Q1 earnings with 38% revenue growth and 57% data center segment growth, driven by accelerating demand for EPYC CPUs used in AI inference and agentic systems. CEO Lisa Su doubled the company's CPU market growth forecast to over 35% annualized through 2030, signaling a structural shift in the business. Despite trading at 35x 2027 earnings, AMD's PEG ratio of 0.69 suggests the stock remains undervalued relative to its 51% expected annualized earnings growth.

items.title: 3 Brilliant Growth Stocks to Buy Now and Hold for the Long Term
items.summary: The article highlights three growth stocks positioned for long-term transformation: Warby Parker is evolving into a holistic vision care platform with AI-powered smart glasses; Cava Group is expanding rapidly with strong unit economics and 38.9% digital revenue penetration; Dutch Bros is leveraging a 74% loyalty program participation rate and testing food offerings to drive growth beyond beverages.

items.title: Alphabet vs. Microsoft: What Recent Revenue Trends Reveal
items.summary: Alphabet and Microsoft both demonstrate strong year-over-year revenue growth driven by AI investments. Alphabet maintains a larger revenue footprint ($109.9B vs $82.9B in Q1 2026) primarily from its dominant Google search engine (90% market share), while Microsoft focuses on cloud computing as its growth engine. Both companies are well-positioned for continued expansion as they integrate AI into their core businesses.

items.title: This Under-the-Radar Growth Stock Just Earned a Promotion
items.summary: Life360, a family safety app used on 1 in 7 U.S. smartphones, reported strong Q1 2026 earnings with 38% revenue growth—more than double its 17% user growth. The company's premium subscription business (Paying Circles) grew 27% year-over-year to 3 million accounts, while its newly highlighted advertising business quadrupled in revenue to $19.7 million following the Nativo acquisition. Despite margin compression from scaling operations, Life360 raised full-year 2026 guidance to 33-40% revenue growth.

items.title: The Race to $10 Trillion: Nvidia vs. Alphabet
items.summary: Nvidia and Alphabet are competing to become the first company to reach a $10 trillion market valuation. Nvidia, currently valued at $5.2 trillion, leads as the world's largest company due to surging demand for its AI chips, with the upcoming Vera Rubin platform expected to drive further growth. Alphabet, valued at $4.8 trillion, is leveraging AI to enhance Google Search and Google Cloud while developing its own TPU chips. While Nvidia appears more likely to reach $10 trillion first based on current momentum and valuation multiples, Alphabet has a more sustainable long-term outlook due to its diversified business model and more stable P/E ratio.

items.title: What Happens to Taiwan Semiconductor Stock if the AI Build-Out Slows Down? Here's My Answer.
items.summary: TSMC's stock has surged nearly 150% over the past year, driven by robust AI demand from hyperscalers. While Q1 2026 results showed strong 40.6% revenue growth and management raised full-year guidance, the stock's rich 35x P/E valuation leaves little room for error. The author warns that if AI infrastructure spending from a handful of major U.S. hyperscalers slows, TSMC could face significant headwinds, rating the stock a 'hold' rather than a 'buy' given the risks.

items.title: Better Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock: Alphabet or Microsoft
items.summary: The article compares Alphabet and Microsoft as AI hyperscalers investing heavily in cloud computing infrastructure. While Alphabet demonstrates stronger growth metrics (22% revenue growth vs. 18% for Microsoft) and advantages in cloud computing with its TPU chips, Microsoft emerges as the better investment due to significantly cheaper valuation. Alphabet trades at decade-high levels while Microsoft is near decade lows, making Microsoft the more attractive option despite Alphabet appearing as the healthier company.

items.title: Uber's $10 Billion Robotaxi Push Could Challenge Tesla And Waymo Dominance
items.summary: Uber is committing $10 billion to its robotaxi ambitions, allocating $2.5 billion for equity investments in robotaxi operators and $7.5 billion for fleet expansion starting in 2027, targeting over 100,000 vehicles. The company is partnering with multiple manufacturers including Lucid, Nuro, and others, leveraging NVIDIA technology. CEO Dara Khosrowshahi views the autonomous vehicle sector as a trillion-dollar market opportunity. Investor Gary Black predicts Uber could lead the global robotaxi sector over Tesla and Waymo due to its operational leverage and massive user base of 200 million monthly active customers.

items.title: A wealth advisor said goodbye to a tech ETF but not to the sector
items.summary: Resolute Wealth Strategies liquidated its entire position in JPMorgan U.S. Tech Leaders ETF (JTEK), selling 34,133 shares worth approximately $2.93 million in May 2026. However, the firm maintained its overall technology sector exposure through individual large-cap tech stocks and other sector-tilted ETFs, suggesting this was a strategic portfolio restructuring rather than a bearish tech call.

items.title: Alphabet’s AI-Focused Debt Raise and Cloud Surge Could Be A Game Changer For Alphabet (GOOGL) - Yahoo Finance
items.summary: Alphabet’s AI-Focused Debt Raise and Cloud Surge Could Be A Game Changer For Alphabet (GOOGL)    Yahoo Finance

items.title: Jensen Huang Just Made a Major Announcement. Here's What It Means for Nvidia Investors.
items.summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang announced the creation of Nvidia Ising, the world's first family of open-source quantum AI models. Rather than building quantum computing hardware like competitors, Nvidia is positioning itself to control the software 'brains' of quantum systems. While quantum computing remains years away from commercial viability, this move demonstrates Nvidia's long-term strategic vision beyond its current AI GPU dominance.

items.title: Missed April's AI Rally? These 3 Stocks Still Look Like Bargains.
items.summary: Despite April's AI sector rally, three megacap tech stocks remain attractive bargains. Microsoft trades 22% below its October high despite strong 18% revenue growth and 40% Azure growth. Nvidia, though near all-time highs, could see 40% upside as companies increase 2027 capital expenditures. Meta offers undervalued AI exposure with 33% revenue growth, trading below market multiples while growing faster than average.

items.title: 2 Stocks With Monster Potential to Hold Through the Next Decade of Chaos
items.summary: The article recommends Cameco and Alphabet as two stocks well-positioned to thrive amid global chaos and market volatility. Cameco, the world's second-largest uranium miner, benefits from accelerating nuclear energy demand with 75 reactors under construction and 120 planned. The company reported strong Q1 2026 results with 7% revenue growth and 88% EPS surge. Alphabet, positioned on both hardware and software sides of AI, grew Q1 2026 revenue 22% to $109.8B and EPS 82% to $5.11, with expanding profit margins.

items.title: Want SpaceX Stock Before the IPO? These 3 Plays Give You a Back Door In.
items.summary: SpaceX is preparing for an IPO expected in early June 2026, with a potential valuation of $2 trillion. Three publicly traded companies offer indirect exposure to SpaceX: Alphabet (6% stake), Bank of America (less than 1% stake), and EchoStar (potential 2.8% stake pending regulatory approval). EchoStar's potential SpaceX position could be worth around $56 billion if the deal is approved.

items.title: Computer Vision in Healthcare Market Report 2026: Revenue Expected to Surpass $33.4 Billion by 2036
items.summary: The global computer vision in healthcare market is projected to grow from $4.37 billion in 2026 to $33.4 billion by 2036, at a CAGR of 22.6%. Growth is driven by AI-integrated diagnostic systems, radiologist shortages, and precision medicine adoption. Software and medical imaging segments lead the market, while Asia-Pacific is expected to see the fastest regional growth.

items.title: Top AST SpaceMobile Insider Cuts Position by Over 30%
items.summary: Rakuten CEO Hiroshi Mikitani has aggressively reduced AST SpaceMobile holdings by approximately 32%, selling $271 million worth of shares in Q2 2026. While still holding over 21 million shares, Rakuten's position has fallen below the 10% threshold. Meanwhile, Marvell Technology sees increased insider selling amid strong stock performance, though most sales are pre-planned. e.l.f. Beauty insider sales appear procedural and non-concerning.

items.title: 1 Unstoppable Stock to Buy Before It Joins Nvidia, Alphabet, Microsoft, and Apple in the $3 Trillion Club
items.summary: Meta Platforms is leveraging artificial intelligence to transform Facebook and Instagram into personalized content hubs, driving increased user engagement and advertising revenue. With a market cap of $1.55 trillion and trading at a P/E ratio of 22.1 (below the Nasdaq-100 average of 33.9), Meta could potentially double in value to reach $3 trillion by next year. The company's Q1 2026 revenue grew 33% year-over-year to $56.3 billion, though significant capital expenditures of up to $145 billion in 2026 for AI infrastructure will impact near-term earnings.

items.title: Broadcom Valuation Tests Limits as AI Revenue Targets Stretch Expectations
items.summary: Broadcom faces a critical test between aggressive AI revenue growth targets and valuation concerns. CEO Hock Tan projects $100 billion in AI chip revenue for 2027 (2.7x total 2025 revenue), with Q2 guidance of $22 billion implying 46.6% YoY growth and $10.7 billion in AI revenue. The company has secured supply chain commitments and diversified customer base including Google, Meta, OpenAI, and Anthropic. However, the stock's P/E of 80.60 and proximity to all-time highs raise questions about whether execution can justify current valuations.

items.title: Great News: Broadcom Just Strengthened Its AI Growth Story
items.summary: Broadcom has emerged as a hidden winner in AI infrastructure with improving revenue, margins, and contract visibility. However, the stock's significant rally has raised concerns about valuation, as the current price may already reflect substantial optimism about future growth.

items.title: Got $1,000? Here Are Three Incredible AI Stocks to Get Started
items.summary: The article recommends three AI stocks for beginner investors with $1,000: Nvidia, a semiconductor giant benefiting from massive AI computing demand with 73% YoY revenue growth; Alphabet, leveraging AI through Google Cloud (63% YoY growth) and Gemini integration; and Nebius, a high-growth neocloud company expecting 523% revenue growth in 2026, though with higher risk.

items.title: Alphabet Is Dropping $190 Billion on AI Infrastructure: 3 Semiconductor Stocks Set to Win Big
items.summary: Alphabet increased its capital expenditure guidance to $180-$190 billion with plans for even higher spending in 2027, signaling continued momentum in AI infrastructure investment. This spending surge is expected to benefit semiconductor companies Broadcom, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC), and Nvidia, which supply chips and processors for AI data centers.

items.title: StellarSpace stellt bei einer Auftaktveranstaltung im Silicon Valley Produkte der neuen Generation für die industrielle Automatisierung vor
items.summary: StellarSpace, a global leader in industrial automation, launched its full-stack industrial intelligence product portfolio at the Global Embodied Intelligence AI Innovation Summit in Silicon Valley on April 28, 2026. The company introduced next-generation PLCs, servo systems, frequency converters, and industrial motors designed for AI-driven industrial applications. CEO Tan Cong highlighted the company's role in providing 'muscles and nerves' for embodied robots and noted international customer orders from multiple regions including the USA, Denmark, Italy, Turkey, UAE, Malaysia, Indonesia, and CIS countries.

items.title: This Investment Gives You Access to All the Hottest Market Trends: Generative AI, SpaceX, Quantum Computing, Robotaxis, and More
items.summary: Alphabet is positioned as a comprehensive investment vehicle providing exposure to multiple cutting-edge technology trends including generative AI, quantum computing, robotaxis, and SpaceX. The company's Gemini AI models compete at the industry frontier, Google Cloud revenue grew 36% in 2025, quantum computing research demonstrated significant breakthroughs, and its Waymo robotaxi subsidiary expanded to 11 cities with a $126 billion valuation. Despite strong performance and growth prospects, Alphabet trades at a relatively attractive 29.5x forward earnings multiple.

items.title: Stock Market Today, April 30: Meta Falls as Higher AI Capex Outlook Overshadows Strong Q1 Results
items.summary: Meta's stock fell 8.6% despite beating Q1 earnings expectations, as investors reacted negatively to the company's sharply increased AI and data center capital expenditure guidance, potentially reaching $145 billion in 2026. The market is concerned whether Meta's ad pricing, engagement, and Reels monetization improvements can offset the rising infrastructure costs and protect margins.

items.title: Stock Market Today, April 30: U.S. Markets Surge Higher Despite a Mixed Bag of Earnings From Big Tech
items.summary: U.S. markets rose on April 30, 2026, with the S&P 500 gaining 1.02%, Nasdaq up 0.89%, and Dow jumping 1.62%. Alphabet surged 10% on strong earnings and AI commentary, while Microsoft and Meta declined 4% and 9% respectively due to concerns over massive AI capital expenditure. Caterpillar also rallied 10% on robust results, helping fuel the Dow's gains.

items.title: 3 Stocks I'd Rather Own Than Apple With a CEO Shakeup
items.summary: With Tim Cook stepping down as Apple CEO in September 2026 and John Ternus taking over, the article argues that leadership transitions create uncertainty. Apple trades at a premium valuation (34x earnings) without strong growth prospects and lacks a clear AI strategy. The author recommends Nvidia, Microsoft, and Alphabet as better investment alternatives due to their strong AI exposure, cloud computing growth, and more attractive valuations.

items.title: Microsoft's Cloud Beat Lands, But AI Stock Laggard Still Has Something To Prove
items.summary: Microsoft delivered better-than-expected Q3 earnings with Azure revenue growing 40% and improved operating margins, but the stock fell 5.95% as investors remain skeptical. Despite strong cloud and AI infrastructure demand, experts note Microsoft has underperformed expectations as an AI winner, with concerns about hardware capacity constraints and lack of meaningful surprises to drive sentiment change.

items.title: Microsoft Doubles Down On Agentic AI, Custom Chips And Data Center Surge As Q3 Results Shine
items.summary: Microsoft reported strong Q3 results with AI business surpassing $37 billion in annual recurring revenue. CEO Satya Nadella outlined the company's strategy centered on 'agentic computing' and announced plans to double data center footprint within two years. Microsoft is investing in custom silicon (Maia 200 and Cobalt) while integrating hardware from partners, achieving 40% improvement in AI inference throughput. The company is scaling AI platforms like Foundry and Fabric with strong enterprise adoption.

items.title: Google 'Proudly' Defends Pentagon AI Ties Amid Internal Backlash: Report
items.summary: Google's leadership defended the company's partnership with the Pentagon to develop AI for military use, despite over 600 employees urging CEO Sundar Pichai to halt the collaboration. Alphabet president Kent Walker stated Google supports defense agencies 'proudly' and responsibly, citing past involvement in classified government work. However, employees and DeepMind researchers have raised concerns that reduced oversight of advanced AI could enable dangerous uses including domestic surveillance and autonomous weapons.

items.title: Elon Musk Can Only Be Fired By Himself At SpaceX, Filing Shows Ahead Of IPO
items.summary: SpaceX's IPO filings reveal that CEO Elon Musk can only be removed from his position by himself, as he holds Class B shares with 10 voting rights each. The dual-class share structure would allow Musk to control board elections and removals if he maintains significant Class B shareholdings, effectively making him irremovable without his consent. This structure limits investor influence over corporate matters.

items.title: Q1 2026 revenues
items.summary: Capgemini reported Q1 2026 revenues of €5,943 million, up 11.0% at constant exchange rates, driven by strong cloud and AI strategy execution. The company secured major deals including a five-year McDonald's partnership extension and achieved 1.02 book-to-bill ratio with generative AI bookings representing over 11% of total bookings. The group targets 2026 revenue growth of 6.5%-8.5% at constant exchange rates with an operating margin of 13.6%-13.8%.

items.title: SpaceX Will Pay Elon Musk To Conquer Mars — No, Really, It's In The Filing
items.summary: SpaceX's board has approved a massive stock award for Elon Musk contingent on achieving extraordinary milestones: a $7.5 trillion company valuation and establishing a permanent human settlement on Mars with one million residents. The 200 million super-voting restricted shares represent one of the largest executive compensation packages ever, though Musk's own timeline suggests Mars colonization is still decades away. This comes as SpaceX prepares for what could be the largest IPO in U.S. history, valued around $1.75 trillion.

items.title: Meta, Google, TikTok Face Australian Crackdown With New Revenue Tax Plan To Protect Struggling News Industry
items.summary: Australia is proposing a new revenue-based tax on major digital platforms including Meta, Google, and TikTok, requiring them to pay a 2.25% levy on Australian revenue unless they strike commercial deals with news organizations. The 'News Bargaining Incentive' aims to support struggling media outlets and ensure journalists are compensated for content. The government estimates the measure could raise AUD 200-250 million annually. Tech giants have pushed back, calling it a disguised digital services tax.

items.title: Alphabet ups 2026 capex to as much as $190 billion, expects to 'significantly increase' in 2027 - CNBC
items.summary: Alphabet ups 2026 capex to as much as $190 billion, expects to 'significantly increase' in 2027    CNBC

items.title: Is There Too Much Hype Baked Into Nvidia's Stock?
items.summary: While Nvidia's trailing P/E ratio of 42x appears expensive, its forward P/E of 25x reflects expected earnings growth of nearly 100% this year. With Wall Street projecting 31% revenue growth next year and the AI infrastructure buildout expected to continue for several years, the author argues Nvidia is not overvalued relative to the broader market and remains a safe investment despite recent gains.

items.title: The AI Supercycle Isn't Slowing Down -- It Just Shifted. These 2 Stocks Are Riding the Next Wave.
items.summary: The AI supercycle is entering a new phase driven by physical AI, agentic AI, and inference applications. Nvidia and Palantir Technologies are positioned to capitalize on these emerging opportunities. Nvidia generated over $6 billion in physical AI revenue last year with a potential $3.25 trillion market by 2040, while Palantir leads in agentic AI solutions with a 46% expected annual growth rate through 2030.

items.title: Nvidia, AMD, and Other AI Chip Stocks Are Swooning. Blame OpenAI.
items.summary: AI chip stocks tumbled after reports that OpenAI's growth is slowing, with the company missing internal targets for user and revenue growth. However, the article argues the sell-off is overdone, as demand for AI is being redistributed among competing platforms rather than declining overall. With strong current revenue growth and attractive valuations, the broader AI market remains healthy.

items.title: Broadcom Hits $2 Trillion Market Cap as Google Reveals New AI Chips
items.summary: Broadcom reached a $2 trillion market capitalization following Google's announcement of its eighth-generation TPU featuring two specialized chips—one for training and one for inference. This development validates Broadcom CEO Hock Tan's earlier predictions about customers developing dual chips annually and strengthens Broadcom's position as a key hardware partner for hyperscalers like Google and Meta, potentially increasing revenue opportunities.

items.title: 1 Glorious Growth Stock Down 30% to Buy on the Dip in 2026
items.summary: Shopify stock has declined over 30% amid investor concerns about SaaS and consumer stocks, but the article argues this presents a buying opportunity. The company is positioned as a leader in agentic AI commerce, demonstrated strong Q4 revenue growth of 31% to $3.67 billion, and expects continued momentum in 2026. Trading at a 11x forward P/S ratio, the stock is considered fairly valued for its growth prospects.

items.title: Google Is Getting a Screaming Bargain on Its New Anthropic Investment. Here's Why.
items.summary: Alphabet is investing up to $40 billion in AI company Anthropic at a $350 billion valuation, starting with a $10 billion initial investment. The article argues this represents a bargain valuation given Anthropic's explosive revenue growth from $1 billion (end of 2024) to $30 billion (April 2026), trading at under 12x sales. The deal also commits 5 gigawatts of computing capacity to Anthropic, which could de-risk Alphabet's massive AI infrastructure spending plans.

items.title: Prediction: Alphabet's New TPUs Are Another Reason to Buy the Stock
items.summary: Alphabet unveiled its eighth-generation Tensor Processing Units (TPUs), with new versions specifically designed for training and inference. The TPU 8i offers 80% better performance-per-dollar and includes enhanced memory features for reduced data transfer lag. The company's custom AI chips provide a significant structural cost advantage over competitors reliant on Nvidia's GPUs, positioning Alphabet as a leader in the AI race with multiple revenue streams through Google Cloud and Broadcom partnerships.

items.title: 1 No-Brainer Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock to Buy With $5,000 and Hold for the Long Term
items.summary: Alphabet is recommended as a compelling long-term AI investment due to its strong financial position ($132.2 billion net income, $127 billion in cash), diversified revenue streams from advertising and Google Cloud (which grew 48% YoY), and strategic AI ventures including Gemini (750M+ monthly active users) and Waymo (self-driving vehicles). The company's ability to invest long-term in new opportunities, demonstrated by Google Cloud's decade-long path to profitability, positions it well for future AI growth.

items.title: Is Alphabet Stock a Buy Ahead of Earnings?
items.summary: Alphabet is set to report Q1 2026 earnings on April 29, with investors closely watching Google Cloud's revenue growth and earnings per share. Google Cloud showed strong momentum with 48% YoY revenue growth in Q4 and operating margins expanding to 30.1%. However, Alphabet's aggressive AI spending plan—with capex expected to roughly double to $175-185 billion in 2026—raises concerns about depreciation pressure offsetting cloud growth benefits. While the stock appears attractive long-term given competitive advantages, the massive capital expenditure bet on AI durability presents elevated risk.

items.title: Why Intel Stock Skyrocketed Today
items.summary: Intel's stock surged 23.6% after reporting strong Q1 earnings that beat Wall Street expectations. The chipmaker's data center and AI division revenue jumped 22% to $5.1 billion, with adjusted net income surging 156% to $1.5 billion. Intel is capitalizing on rising demand for CPUs driven by agentic AI, with new partnerships from Google, Tesla, and other tech leaders expected to fuel further growth.

items.title: Is the Memory Supercycle Already Priced In? What the Data Says About Micron Stock.
items.summary: Micron Technology is positioned to benefit significantly from the AI-driven memory supercycle, with the company experiencing explosive growth (196% YoY revenue increase in Q2 FY2026) and projecting 260% YoY growth for Q3. Despite a 585% stock surge over the past year, the article argues the memory supercycle is not fully priced in, citing Micron's low PEG ratio of 0.46 and strong fundamentals. The memory shortage is expected to persist until 2030, with only three companies (Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron) dominating the market.
